title: A few questions on being tanged
display_title: A few questions on being tanged
wikitext:
[]: A few questions on being tanged             Quote Post by Chroma » Thu Sep 06, 2012 9:25 pm As far as I know, the rule for being tanged is that you see the person you love the most instead of the multitude of Rei/Liliths as they tangify you. Now, there are truly people in the world who love themselves more than anything - would they see themselves before they were tanged? And what if someone who was tanged loved Rei the most (lets say a student from her class) - would they actually see the Rei/Lilith beings? Would they see Rei as a student? In all actuality, I'm confused over how the tanging actually happens. The way I see it is that a person loses their individuality when they fully accept the person they love (whoever shows up to tang them), which would be releasing their AT fields. But what's *actually* happening? What are the Rei/Liliths doing?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Sep 06, 2012 9:31 pm Aoba saw a bunch of Reis dragging him out from his desk. Assuming that his heart's desire isn't a room full of naked Rei Ayanamis (Aynamii?) his self-centered sarcastic nature left him unable to conjure a welcoming image, so Lilith just brute forced it until he gave up.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Sep 06, 2012 9:57 pm It all goes back to the AT Field. It's what gives people a physical and mental boundary, what separates them from other people. When they are in love with someone, that person is able to push through their AT Field to get closer to the soul, the true self. Rei/Lilith takes advantage of this fact to make the assimilation of her "children" as painless as possible. She uses the images of people's loved ones to make them let down their AT Fields willingly and allow the soul to escape. Once the soul leaves the body, there is nothing there to generate an AT Field. The body is no longer able to hold its shape, so it reverts to a puddle of LCL. It's not quite as simple as just seeing the person you love most, either. You see your ideal image of him/her. Hyuga sees a Misato who is in love with him. Maya sees a Ritsuko who treats her less like a subordinate and more like a lover. Aoba... well, it seems as if he never thought of anyone in that way, so the Reis simply powered through his AT Field to drag his screaming soul into the Egg of Lilith. You could also think of the Reis as a manifestation of the Anti-AT Field that Fuyutsuki mentioned as the MP Evas were setting the stage for Instrumentality. I'm writing this on the fly, so I apologize for any inconsistencies or factual errors. Feel free to skewer this one, guys.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Thu Sep 06, 2012 10:20 pm Chuckman wrote: Aoba saw a bunch of Reis dragging him out from his desk. Assuming that his heart's desire isn't a room full of naked Rei Ayanamis (Aynamii?) his self-centered sarcastic nature left him unable to conjure a welcoming image, so Lilith just brute forced it until he gave up.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Thu Sep 06, 2012 10:38 pm There remains the interesting question of Gendo, who imagined being killed by (a presumably Shinji-piloted) Unit-01. Or, of course, the classic - Shinji seeing a woman's bust before tangifying.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 06, 2012 11:03 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Once the soul leaves the body, there is nothing there to generate an AT Field. The body is no longer able to hold its shape, so it reverts to a puddle of LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by Ceimoa Nan » Thu Sep 06, 2012 11:57 pm I always figured that Gendo's top half exploded or melted into LCL while his bottom half didn't. We know that different body parts can go at different times, like with Ibuki's hand staying together after the rest of her went. And right before Gendo's top half explodes or melts, he "imagines" Yui/Eva-01 picking him up and eating him. I'd think that in reality, Gendo was never picked up, and was either standing or lying down like we saw him right before the vision and liquefying. Since his bottom half is either standing or lying down when we see it. (I can't tell which since my video's quality is crummy.) Edit: Then again, maybe the Eva-01 projection did lift him up. The Rei-as-Ritsuko projection was able to type onto a computer, presumably, unless Maya did that herself in a sort of trance, so I guess these projections can interact with matter. In ways other than turning things into LCL and being perceived by people. (Maybe I'm thinking about something that Anno et al. never really considered. ) Another Edit: I read Reichu's post she linked to, so yeah, Gendo was standing up. As to whether he was actually "eaten" by Yui's Eva-01 projection, and his top half was teleported into Eva-01's belly or core or whatever as Reichu thinks, or if his top half was liquefied, as I thought, I still can't decide. In the picture, it could be blood or LCL that's on his remains, I think. But there's no LCL pooling at his feet. No blood either. Hm.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Sep 07, 2012 1:03 am I personally take the global crossplossion extravaganza as her dealing with any and all stubborn cases all at once. "You won't come willingly? Fine, we won't be needing this planet anymore anyway..." Gendo surely was killed somehow, though making concrete sense of the scene is probably an unwinnable battle. The real question to my mind is whether his little red dot was allowed into instrumentality with everyone else or if it was somehow destroyed; iow, is his 'punnishment' that he doesn't get a pleasant final send-off or that instrumentality is the ancient order of the no-gendos club?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 07, 2012 1:45 am Ceimoa Nan wrote: In the picture, it could be blood or LCL that's on his remains, I think.

[]:             Quote Post by flapjack » Fri Sep 07, 2012 2:43 am bump

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Fri Sep 07, 2012 4:54 am Reichu wrote: Imagined? He was killed. His entire top half was bitten off. The Eva-01 there visually beckons back to Yui's awakening in #19. Also, check out the Yui's shirt-changing maneuver. (In fact, that's a good thread for debating what the fuck is going on in that scene.)

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Fri Sep 07, 2012 5:09 am flapjack wrote: The only reason he would receive punishment would be because he personally victimized Rei. For that, I believe he was punished accordingly by Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Sep 07, 2012 11:41 am Gendo stuff can go here: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/1840/Gendos-Mysterious-Induction-or-How-I-Ate-My-Husband/0/?

It's what gives people a physical and mental boundary, what separates them from other people. When they are in love with someone, that person is able to push through their AT Field to get closer to the soul, the true self. Rei/Lilith takes advantage of this fact to make the assimilation of her "children" as painless as possible. She uses the images of people's loved ones to make them let down their AT Fields willingly and allow the soul to escape. Once the soul leaves the body, there is nothing there to generate an AT Field. Aoba... well, it seems as if he never thought of anyone in that way, so the Reis simply powered through his AT Field to drag his screaming soul into the Egg of Lilith. You could also think of the Reis as a manifestation of the Anti-AT Field that Fuyutsuki mentioned as the MP Evas were setting the stage for Instrumentality.
